questions.question: Explain the difference between a physical clock and a logical clock in the context of distributed systems. Provide an example of how a logical clock can be used to resolve conflicts between concurrent processes.
questions.hint: Focus on the conceptual differences and provide a concrete scenario.

questions.question: Consider a distributed system with three processes P1, P2, and P3, each running on separate machines. If each process increments a shared variable 'x' once per second, what will be the value of 'x' after 3 seconds? Explain why this is the case, and describe the logical clock used to reason about the system.
questions.hint: Focus on the order of events and the logical clock's role in resolving concurrent updates.

questions.question: A distributed system uses a logical clock with a maximum increment value of 10. Process P1 sets the clock value to 5, and process P2 sets the clock value to 8. What happens when process P3 tries to compare the clock values of P1 and P2? Explain the outcome and describe the implications for the system's consistency.
questions.hint: Focus on the clock values and the comparison operation.
